jokes.text: Too wonderful. You're right; this is it. They're selling us to be crash test dummies! Oh, please let it be volvo.
jokes.setup: Homer takes the kids for ice cream and kittens, creating suspicion about hidden motives
jokes.punchline: The kids' paranoid conclusion is hilariously specific - crash test dummies for Volvo specifically
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurdist paranoia with the kids' hilariously specific fear and Homer's brand preference even in dire circumstances

jokes.text: it's shot day! welcome to hell... man avenue medical plaza.
jokes.setup: Medical staff announces shot day with ominous tone
jokes.punchline: The medical plaza name sounds like 'Hellman Avenue' creating a pun about medical visits being hellish
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay joke that works but isn't particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: children, you should be grateful you live in a country where childhood diseases have been practically... Karate!
jokes.setup: Doctor begins earnest speech about vaccination benefits
jokes.punchline: Bart interrupts with karate move, completely derailing the medical lecture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart behavior - impulsive physical comedy interrupting adult seriousness. Great economy and character integration

jokes.text: That hobo skeleton is not a toy!
jokes.setup: Medical office has anatomical skeleton for educational purposes
jokes.punchline: Doctor refers to it as a 'hobo skeleton' suggesting its questionable origins
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny implication about where medical skeletons come from, very quotable line

jokes.text: Only babies and ex-junkies are afraid of needles. Stick me, chuckles!
jokes.setup: Bart acts tough about getting a shot
jokes.punchline: His bravado is undermined by calling the doctor 'chuckles' and we know he'll soon run away
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's false bravado before his inevitable cowardice

jokes.text: Don't forget maggie! I got her when your heads were turned. Got her good!
jokes.setup: Family thinks they're done with shots
jokes.punchline: Doctor reveals he secretly injected baby Maggie while they were distracted
jokes.explanation: Mildly dark humor about medical efficiency, reasonably structured surprise

jokes.text: No, thanks. I already helped myself.
jokes.setup: Doctor offers Bart lollipops after his shot
jokes.punchline: Bart reveals he's already stolen a bunch, showing his mouth full or pockets stuffed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart behavior - taking advantage of any situation for personal gain

jokes.text: see you later, inoculator!
jokes.setup: Bart makes his escape from the doctor's office
jokes.punchline: His parting quip rhymes 'later' with 'inoculator' in classic Bart style
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart one-liner with good wordplay and character voice

jokes.text: he can run, but nobody escapes the needle. Darlene, cancel all my appointments. But I need that kidney now.
jokes.setup: Doctor dramatically vows to catch Bart for his shot
jokes.punchline: The escalation reveals the doctor cancels appointments including someone waiting for a kidney transplant
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from routine shot to life-or-death surgery, showing absurd priorities

jokes.text: black-on-black violence must end. That was for dr. King.
jokes.setup: Bart apparently destroys something black (likely a blackboard)
jokes.punchline: He frames his vandalism as a civil rights statement honoring MLK
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart rationalization of misbehavior with pseudo-intellectual justification

jokes.text: Don't feel bad, doc. I won't even let my mom clip my toenails.
jokes.setup: Doctor is frustrated about failing to give Bart his shot
jokes.punchline: Bart reveals his extreme aversion to any medical/grooming procedures
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that explains Bart's behavior while being relatable

jokes.text: Okay, well, I really enjoyed being you, dr. Hibbert. Oh, by the way, you're not welcome in the library no more.
jokes.setup: Moe was apparently disguised as Dr. Hibbert to help catch Bart
jokes.punchline: He casually mentions banning the real doctor from the library while impersonating him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - using his temporary authority to settle personal grudges. Great character integration

jokes.text: I'm afraid the inoculation has swollen his ear holes shut.
jokes.setup: Bart suddenly can't hear after getting his shot
jokes.punchline: Medical diagnosis using childish term 'ear holes' instead of 'ear canals'
jokes.explanation: Simple humor from medical professional using unprofessional terminology

jokes.text: If it doesn't, call me in the bahamas.
jokes.setup: Doctor says Bart's hearing should return in a day
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's immediately going on vacation, abandoning responsibility
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing medical professional's casual attitude toward malpractice

jokes.text: That was a malpractice waiver, fool!
jokes.setup: Doctor gives Bart a 'complimentary pen' to try out
jokes.punchline: Reveals Bart just signed away his legal rights unknowingly
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with dark implications about medical practice, very well structured

jokes.text: Give your wallet, kid! We're gonna identity theft your ass!
jokes.setup: Bullies threaten Bart who can't hear them
jokes.punchline: Modern crime threat using contemporary terminology in old-fashioned mugging format
jokes.explanation: Good contemporary reference mixing old and new criminal threats

jokes.text: he's just standing his ground! He knows that deep down, all bullies are cowards! We're undone!
jokes.setup: Bart appears to courageously face down the bullies
jokes.punchline: Reality is he just can't hear their threats, but they interpret it as brave defiance
jokes.explanation: Great ironic situation where Bart's disability is mistaken for courage, well-constructed

jokes.text: Willie's been deaf since the boiler explosion of '88. But I taught myself to read lips.
jokes.setup: Willie reveals he understands Bart's situation
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of workplace injury that caused permanent disability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie backstory - workplace accident explained matter-of-factly, great character integration

jokes.text: What'd you say about me mother?! For your information, her feet stink 'cause she works in manure all day!
jokes.setup: Willie misreads lips and gets defensive about his mother
jokes.punchline: His defense actually reveals embarrassing information about his mother
jokes.explanation: Classic Willie overreaction with TMI about his family, good character consistency

jokes.text: But it's still the best darn starbucks in glasgow!
jokes.setup: Willie continues defending his mother's workplace
jokes.punchline: Reveals she works at a Starbucks, anachronistic for his Scottish background
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail mixing Willie's Scottish origins with modern American coffee culture

jokes.text: Bart, pass the ketchup. Bart, pass the ketchup! Dad, he's deaf. Oh, sorry, right. Bart, pass the corn. Bart, pass the corn!
jokes.setup: Homer doesn't understand how deafness works
jokes.punchline: After being corrected, he just asks for something different at the same volume
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely missing the point of deafness. Excellent character integration and escalation

jokes.text: Now I've heard everything. Unlike you!
jokes.setup: Homer reacts to donkey basketball with common expression
jokes.punchline: Immediately makes insensitive pun about Bart's deafness
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer insensitivity delivered with perfect timing and economy

jokes.text: everybody, remember that for when his hearing comes back.
jokes.setup: Homer makes a good joke about Bart's deafness
jokes.punchline: Marge wants to preserve the moment for later appreciation
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor with Marge acknowledging the joke's quality, fits her character

jokes.text: I'm sure that 'mule' enjoy it.
jokes.setup: Announcer introduces donkey basketball game
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun replacing 'you'll' with 'mule' that even he recognizes as bad
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad pun that works because the show acknowledges it's terrible

jokes.text: man, tough crowd.
jokes.setup: Announcer's terrible pun gets no reaction
jokes.punchline: His acknowledgment of the audience's lack of response
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction to failed joke, works as meta-commentary

jokes.text: hey, donkey, want a carrot? Psyche!
jokes.setup: Bart offers carrot to donkey during basketball game
jokes.punchline: He pulls it away at the last second in classic fake-out move
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart mischief, predictable but fits character perfectly

jokes.text: good lord! Bart's mooning the flag! Don't look, buttermilk.
jokes.setup: Bart appears to be disrespecting the flag during national anthem
jokes.punchline: Ned shields his cow's eyes from the inappropriate display
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned reaction with the absurd detail of protecting his cow's innocence

jokes.text: how dare he?! That's the flag my grandpappy rebelled against!
jokes.setup: Cletus is outraged by flag disrespect
jokes.punchline: Reveals his ancestor fought against the very flag he's now defending
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical irony perfectly suited to Cletus's character - Confederate ancestor but modern patriot. Excellent craft and very quotable

jokes.text: When you insult the flag, you insult my flag tattoo!
jokes.setup: Character expresses outrage at flag disrespect
jokes.punchline: Makes it personal by referencing his patriotic body art
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific reaction that escalates the patriotic fervor in personal terms

jokes.text: Your child's behavior appalls me, not just as a principal, but as a veteran of america's only losing war. To date.
jokes.setup: Skinner expresses outrage at Bart's apparent flag disrespect
jokes.punchline: References Vietnam as 'America's only losing war' with ominous qualifier 'to date'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character moment combining his military background with dark political commentary. The 'to date' qualifier is brilliantly ominous

jokes.text: Oh, sure, marge-- just like 'blind bart,' 'wheelchair bart,' 'pregnant bart,' and my personal favorite, 'railroad-spike- through-head bart.'
jokes.setup: Marge explains Bart was deaf and didn't know what he was doing
jokes.punchline: Skinner lists increasingly absurd past excuses for Bart's behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating list building to absurd climax. The specificity and escalation are perfectly crafted, highly quotable

jokes.text: kids love trains.
jokes.setup: Skinner mentions 'railroad-spike-through-head Bart'
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely inappropriate casual response missing the severity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - completely missing the point with child-like logic. Maximum economy and character integration

jokes.text: Cram it, iraqi! Why'd you dis the flag? It partied on the moon!
jokes.setup: Nelson confronts Bart about flag incident
jokes.punchline: His patriotic defense uses childish language about the flag 'partying' on the moon
jokes.explanation: Great Nelson line mixing his aggression with childish understanding of patriotism. 'Partied on the moon' is wonderfully absurd

jokes.text: Weren't we afraid of him just a few days ago? We're complex!
jokes.setup: Bullies now turn against Bart after previously fearing him
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of their inconsistent behavior
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor acknowledging character inconsistency, though not groundbreaking

jokes.text: I want you to overhype this story so much, it makes the new york post look like the new york times. Or the new york times look like the new york post. I forget which one the good one is.
jokes.setup: News director wants to sensationalize the Bart story
jokes.punchline: Can't remember which newspaper represents quality vs. tabloid journalism
jokes.explanation: Good media satire about journalistic standards, though the confusion feels slightly forced

jokes.text: I did not come to this country illegally to see it mocked.
jokes.setup: Apu expresses patriotic outrage at Bart's actions
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to illegal immigration while defending American values
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic line perfectly suited to Apu's character. Great economy and memorable phrasing

jokes.text: There's the boy who bared his bottom to old glory. And the family that applauds his every moonery.
jokes.setup: TV report describes Bart's flag incident
jokes.punchline: Creates the word 'moonery' as if it's Bart's regular practice
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay creating 'moonery' though the overall joke is fairly standard media sensationalism

jokes.text: They hate our country, but they love our soapy water!
jokes.setup: TV report continues attacking the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Points out they still use American car wash services despite their supposed anti-Americanism
jokes.explanation: Decent media hypocrisy joke but not particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Hey, benedict arnold, our cups don't want your colorful balls. No one calls me unpatriotic.
jokes.setup: Homer is banned from children's play area
jokes.punchline: Innocent description of ball pit becomes unintentionally suggestive
jokes.explanation: Mild double entendre that works within context, plus good Homer patriotic reaction

jokes.text: Hey, how ya doing, homer? Oh, not too bad. How about you? Oh, I'm fine. Your wife was great in bed last night. You keep your hands off my wife! Oh, yeah? Well, I give her what she needs.
jokes.setup: Homer talks to himself when ignored at the bar
jokes.punchline: His imaginary conversation escalates to him getting jealous of himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer moment showing his loneliness and stupidity. Perfect escalation to self-jealousy. Near-perfect character integration

jokes.text: This bar's only for real americans. And people on permanent visas, like me. What? What are you all looking at? I'm dutch.
jokes.setup: Moe declares the bar for real Americans only
jokes.punchline: Immediately makes exception for visa holders, then customer defensively claims Dutch heritage
jokes.explanation: Good sequence about xenophobic hypocrisy with typical Moe contradictions

jokes.text: I'm gonna go listen to the president's weekly radio address. And not the rebuttal! Good-bye, homer. I can't get drunk and vomit next to a guy I don't respect.
jokes.setup: Barney declares his patriotism and leaves the bar
jokes.punchline: His moral standards about drinking companionship despite being an alcoholic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney line showing his drunken dignity and unexpected principles

jokes.text: Son, I've learned one thing in this life: To stay out of trouble, just say nothing. Don't rock the boat. Don't even get in the boat. Just buy some ice cream and walk around the pier. But don't go in the bathrooms, they're filthy.
jokes.setup: Homer gives Bart advice about avoiding controversy
jokes.punchline: His wisdom becomes increasingly specific and mundane, ending with bathroom cleanliness
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer wisdom that starts philosophical and becomes hilariously practical. Perfect character voice and escalation

jokes.text: Talk about boats! Then when he asks you if you hate this country, you just start crying and pretend you don't. But I don't hate this country. There, there, save your lies for the american public.
jokes.setup: Homer gives Bart media strategy advice
jokes.punchline: Assumes Bart is lying about loving America and should save deception for TV
jokes.explanation: Good Homer cynicism about media manipulation and his assumption about Bart's true feelings

jokes.text: Bart simpson, what do you hate most about this country? Is it the freedom?
jokes.setup: TV host interviews Bart about the flag incident
jokes.punchline: Completely loaded question assuming guilt and offering absurd options
jokes.explanation: Good media satire of loaded interview questions, though fairly predictable

jokes.text: all right, so if I hear you correctly, you're saying america is better than jesus. Do you agree?
jokes.setup: Bart gives patriotic response mentioning Jesus and military
jokes.punchline: Host completely twists words into impossible theological controversy
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of how media twists innocent statements into controversy

jokes.text: If by 'us', you mean loudmouthed talk show hosts-- which everyone seems to be in this country-- then yes, I do hate americans!
jokes.setup: Host asks if Marge hates 'us' (Americans)
jokes.punchline: Marge's clarification accidentally creates perfect soundbite confirming the accusation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing how reasonable positions can be twisted into damaging soundbites. Great structure

jokes.text: There you have it-- springfield hates america. Now, coming up after this commercial, I will be talking even louder. Don't miss it!
jokes.setup: Host twists Marge's words into town-wide condemnation
jokes.punchline: Promises to escalate his already excessive volume for next segment
jokes.explanation: Good media satire about sensationalism and volume over substance

jokes.text: I declare today, december 25, 'we hate springfield day.'
jokes.setup: Official response to Springfield controversy
jokes.punchline: Declares anti-Springfield day specifically on Christmas
jokes.explanation: Good irony of choosing Christmas for a hate day, though not groundbreaking

jokes.text: Look what happened to Hitler City, North Carolina. If they hadn't changed their name to Charlotte, they'd be sunk.
jokes.setup: Discussion about changing Springfield's name to avoid controversy
jokes.punchline: Implies Charlotte, NC was originally named Hitler City
jokes.explanation: Absurdist alternative history that's darkly funny and memorable

jokes.text: $17.76?! I can't afford to sell a westside home for that! But what a fantastical year for pizza by the slice!
jokes.setup: Patriotic fervor affects real estate prices with 1776 reference
jokes.punchline: Luigi sees opportunity in the economic downturn for his pizza business
jokes.explanation: Good economic humor showing how different businesses are affected by the controversy

jokes.text: Come on, kid, go! You got the red!
jokes.setup: Patriotic traffic lights cause confusion
jokes.punchline: Driver doesn't know which color means go in the new system
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the traffic light gag, showing the practical consequences

jokes.text: Oh I don't think so. It is a heartfelt expression of my true desire to protect my mahogany noggin.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests Apu is going overboard with patriotic displays
jokes.punchline: Apu admits it's self-preservation while using elaborate vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu voice with his verbose honesty about self-preservation motives

jokes.text: Poonam and uma are fighting! No, no. Those are their pre-witch hunt names. Their new names are freedom, lincoln, condoleeza, coke, pepsi, manifest destiny, apple pie and superman. And together, we're the macgillicuddys.
jokes.setup: Apu's children are fighting
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's changed all their names to absurdly patriotic concepts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating list of increasingly absurd patriotic names, ending with generic Irish surname. Excellent craft and highly quotable

jokes.text: I'd like to say something about the current climate of repression and fear. Oh, put a fork in it! Sit down, saddam!
jokes.setup: Lisa tries to make thoughtful political statement in church
jokes.punchline: Congregation immediately shouts her down with ethnic slur
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how intellectual discourse is shut down during patriotic fervor

jokes.text: 'Congress shall make no law abridging the freedom of speech, or of the press.' That's from the first amendment to the constitution.
jokes.setup: Lisa continues her defense of free speech
jokes.punchline: Her constitutional knowledge contrasts with the crowd's ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Lisa but fairly straightforward constitutional reference

jokes.text: Simpsons, you're under arrest for the violation of the government knows best act.
jokes.setup: Government arrests the Simpsons for their speech
jokes.punchline: References Orwellian-named legislation that criminalizes dissent
jokes.explanation: Good political satire with perfectly named authoritarian legislation

jokes.text: Elmo go to wrong fund-raiser.
jokes.setup: Elmo is imprisoned with political dissidents
jokes.punchline: His innocent explanation for being in political prison
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor with Elmo's characteristic speech pattern explaining political imprisonment

jokes.text: I called the republican tax cuts unwise. And I stand by it. They're unwise!
jokes.setup: Bill Clinton explains why he's imprisoned
jokes.punchline: His mild criticism of tax policy is treated as treason
jokes.explanation: Good political satire about the extremes of partisan politics

jokes.text: I'm here because I said imported wines are better than napa valley. Yeah? Well, why don't you take a nap-a?
jokes.setup: Prisoner explains imprisonment for wine preference opinion
jokes.punchline: Guard makes terrible pun on 'Napa' while being oppressive
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that works mainly as political satire about trivial reasons for imprisonment

jokes.text: my only crime was driving a truck full of explosives in from canada. I don't know why they put the real ones in with the joke ones.
jokes.setup: Various prisoners explain their trivial 'crimes'
jokes.punchline: One prisoner admits to actual terrorism while Homer notes the mixture of real and fake criminals
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor contrasting actual terrorists with political prisoners, with good Homer observation

jokes.text: I wish I could say the same for my crazy cousin, bill o' rights. What did I do? What did I do?
jokes.setup: Personified Constitution appears in propaganda film
jokes.punchline: Bill of Rights portrayed as mentally unstable family member
jokes.explanation: Clever political satire personifying constitutional documents with family dysfunction

jokes.text: I don't know whether it's the lack of sleep, the sodium pentathol, or that it's the only cartoon I've seen in two months, but this is what I believe in now.
jokes.setup: Homer watches government propaganda cartoon
jokes.punchline: His honest assessment of how brainwashing techniques are affecting him
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer self-awareness about his susceptibility to propaganda, dark but funny

jokes.text: I'm the last registered democrat. Tax and spend! Tax and spend!
jokes.setup: Mysterious prisoner offers to help them escape
jokes.punchline: Reveals identity with stereotypical Democratic slogan
jokes.explanation: Standard political satire using familiar partisan stereotypes

jokes.text: I don't want to be in any stupid talent show. Not without my tennessee walking horses.
jokes.setup: Prisoners must participate in talent show to escape
jokes.punchline: Inmate has very specific performance requirements involving expensive horses
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about specific performance needs in prison context

jokes.text: now, people have accused this family of not loving our country, but you can't spell 'U.S.A.' Without us... eh?
jokes.setup: Homer begins patriotic song performance
jokes.punchline: Weak wordplay connecting 'us' to 'U.S.A.' that even he recognizes as feeble
jokes.explanation: Intentionally weak pun that works because Homer recognizes its weakness

jokes.text: americans are brave and loyal so come on, jerks, give us your oil
jokes.setup: Simpsons perform patriotic song
jokes.punchline: Song becomes honest about American foreign policy motivations
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire embedded in seemingly patriotic song

jokes.text: dad, why are you still singing that stupid song? Because if they catch us, we may have to do it again. And this time, I want it tight.
jokes.setup: Family escapes but Homer continues singing patriotic song
jokes.punchline: Homer's professional perfectionism about their terrible performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic prioritizing performance quality over escape urgency

jokes.text: What genius put a prison on the middle of an island?
jokes.setup: Family realizes they're on an island prison
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically questions obvious prison design (like Alcatraz)
jokes.explanation: Standard observational humor about obvious prison design principles

jokes.text: Hold your fire. That walrus will eat them. - No, that's the dad. But he's eating a seal.
jokes.setup: Guards spot family swimming away
jokes.punchline: Mistake Homer for marine mammal, then note he's actually eating something
jokes.explanation: Great visual humor about Homer's appearance and eating habits even while swimming

jokes.text: Swim toward san francisco! I'm not made of money! We'll swim to oakland.
jokes.setup: Family needs to swim to shore from island prison
jokes.punchline: Homer's concern about real estate costs even during escape
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - worrying about housing costs during life-threatening escape

jokes.text: I don't want to end up like elmo, hanging himself in prison.
jokes.setup: Lisa worries about going back to prison
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of beloved children's character committing suicide
jokes.explanation: Very dark humor about children's character's fate, effective shock value

jokes.text: Come to France and we shall mock the country that saved us twice from the germans. Eh, you wait till you see her up close, then you tell me.
jokes.setup: French offer refuge to anti-American family
jokes.punchline: Admits to ingratitude toward America, plus casual disparagement of Catherine Deneuve
jokes.explanation: Good French stereotype humor combining historical ingratitude with celebrity gossip

jokes.text: The united states has its grandeur and its follies, but, mostly, it's the place where all our stuff is.
jokes.setup: Family discusses missing America while in France
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical reason for homesickness - material possessions
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge line combining philosophical tone with mundane practicality. Very quotable and true to character

jokes.text: Name? - Simpson. From now on,you're the simps! Mm, that'll save time.
jokes.setup: Immigration officer processes returning family
jokes.punchline: Shortens their name, Homer appreciates the efficiency
jokes.explanation: Simple name-change joke with typical Homer practicality
